title: Anchors Away for Navy Day!
story: Every October 27, Americans celebrate Navy Day to honor the brave sailors who protect our seas! People visit naval museums, watch ship parades, and learn about life aboard submarines and aircraft carriers. Many cities host special ceremonies where veterans share exciting stories about their adventures on the ocean. Some families even dress up in sailor uniforms for fun photos!
activity_question: What do people often visit on Navy Day to learn about naval history?
follow_up_question: What would be the most exciting part of serving on a Navy ship?

title: Lights, Camera, Save It Forever!
story: Movies, TV shows, and old radio programs need saving too! On October 27, people around the world celebrate World Day for Audiovisual Heritage by sharing their favorite classic films and vintage recordings online. Libraries and museums host special movie screenings and display ancient film reels that might crumble without proper care.
activity_question: What do people share online during World Day for Audiovisual Heritage?
activity_choices: Their favorite classic films and vintage recordings
activity_answer: Their favorite classic films and vintage recordings
bonus_fact: Some old movies are stored in special freezers to keep them from turning to dust!
